០០២/១៩-៩-២០០៧-អវតក/អជសដ  អនុលោមតាមសាលដីកាលើ
បានទទួល សំណុំរឿងលេខ
បណ្តឹងឧទ្ធរណ៍របស់ អៀង សារី អៀង ធីរិទ្ធ ge ជា និង ខៀវ សំផន ប្រឆាំងនឹងដីកាដំណោះស្រាយ
ដែលបានចេញដោយអង្គបុរេជំនុំជម្រះនៅថ្ងៃទី om ខែ មករា ឆ្នាំ ២០១១។

វានេឃើញ ពាក្យសុំទទួលស្គាល់ មេធាវី គង់ សំអុន ជាមេធាវីការពារក្តីឲឱ្យលោក ខៀវ សំផន នៅ
ក្នងដំណើរការនីតិវិធីរបស់អង្គជំនុំជម្រះសាលាដំបួងនៃ អ.វ.ត.ក ចុះថ្ងៃទី 99 ខែ តុលា ឆ្នាំ ២០១១។

វានឃើញ លិខិតរបស់អង្គភាពគាំពារការពារក្ដី ចុះថ្ងៃទី ២៤ ខៃ តុលា ឆ្នាំ ២០១១ ដែលបានតែង
តាំងលោកមេធាវី គង់ សំអុន ជាមេធាវីការពារក្តីឲឱ្យលោក ខៀវ សំផន។

ហតុដូេច្លះអង្គជិនុំជម្រះសាលាដំបូងសេប្រច:

9.

ទទួលស្គាល់ លោកមេធាវី គង់ សំអុន ជាមេធាវីការពារក្តីឲ្យលោក ខៀវ សំផន
មុខអង្គជំនុំជម្រះសាលាដំបួងរបស់ អ.វ.ត.ក ។

នៅចំពោះ

ំ

សាលដីកាលើបណ្តឹងឧទ្ធរណ៍របស់ អៀង សារី ប្រឆាំងនឹងដីកានំណោះស្រាយ ចុះថ្ងៃទី om ខែ មករា ឆ្នាំ woes
ឯកសារ 1427/1/26។ សាលដីកាលើបណ្ដឹងឧទ្ទរណ៍របស់ អៀង ធីរិទ្ធ និង ge ជា ប្រឆាំងនឹងដីកាដំណោះស្រាយ ចុះថ្ងៃទី
D427/2/129 សាលដីកាលើបណ្តឹងឧទ្ធរណ៍របស់ ខៀវ សំផន ប្រឆាំងនឹងដីកា
om ខែ មករា ឆ្នាំ ២099 ឯកសារ
ដំណោះស្រាយ ចុះថ្ងៃទី om ខែ មករា ឆ្នាំ ២០9១ ឯកសារ 1427/4/14។ និងសាលដីកាលើបណ្តឹងឧទ្ទរណ៍របស់ អៀង
សារី ប្រឆាំងនឹងការសម្រេចបន្តថិរវេលានៃការឃុំខ្លួនគាត់បណ្តោះអាសន្ននៅក្នុងដឹកានំណោះស្រាយ ចុះថ្ងៃទី ១៣ ខែ មករា
ឆ្នាំ ២០១១ ឯកសារ D427/5/9

សេចក្តីសម្រេចទទួលស្គាល់មេធាវី គង់ សំអុន/ថ្ងៃទី ១៨ ខៃ វិច្ឆិកា ឆ្នាំ ២០០១១/សាធារណៈ

@. ។0//

២.

 កត់សំគាល់ថា មេធាវីដែលបានទទួលស្គាល់រ្លួចហើយមានសិទ្ធិនិងកាតព្វកិច្ចជាអាទិ៍ ៖

ក. ការចូលរ្លួមក្នុងដំណើរការនីតិវិធីចំពោះមុខអង្គជំនុំជម្រះសាលាគំបួង ៖ លោក/លោកស្រី ត្រូវ
បានអនុញ្ញាតឲ្យតំណាងកូនក្ដីរបស់ខ្លួន =នៅក្នុងអំឡុងពេលដៃលពួកគេចូលរួមក្នុងដំណើរ
ដួចមានចែងក្នងវិធានផ្ទៃក្នងរបស់
ការនីតិវិធីនៅចំពោះមុខអង្គជំនុំជម្រះសាលាដំបួង
អ.វ.ត.ក ។

. ការពិនិត្យពិគ្រោះសំណុំរឿង៖ លោក/លោកស្រី មានសិទ្ធិពិនិត្ប្យមើល និងថតចម្តងឯកសារ
ពីសំណុំរឿងស៊ើបសួរច្បាប់ដើមណា ដែលកូនក្តីរបស់លោក/លោកស្រីជាដើមបណ្តឹងរដ្ឋប្ប
វេណីតាមរបៀបថតចម្តងជាក្រដាស
ឬតាមបែបអេឡិចត្រូនិក = នៅក្នុងពេលម៉ោងធ្វើការ
និងដោយគោរពទៅតាមលក្ខខណ្ឌតម្រូវនៃកិច្ចដំណើរការល្អរបស់ អ.វ.ត.ក
ការពិនិត្យ
មើលសំណុំរឿងនេះអាចរៀបចំដោយមានជំនួយរបស់អង្គភាពជនរងគ្រោះ ឬមន្ត្រីគ្រប់គ្រង
សំណុំរឿង ប៉ុន្តែត្រូវស្លិតនៅក្រោមការត្រួតពិនិត្យរបស់ក្រឡាបញ្ហីមានសមត្ថកិច្ច ។ លោក/
លោកស្រី អាចថតចម្តង ឬស្នើសុំច្បាប់ថតចម្តងឯកសារដៃលមាននៅក្នុងសំណុំរឿងតាម
និងយកឯកសារទាំងនោះទៅពិភាក្សាជាមួយកូួនក្តីរបស់លោក/លោកស្រី
ចំនួនសមរម្យ
ប៉ុន្តែ លោក/លោកស្រី មិនអាចប្រគល់ឯកសារថតចម្តងទាំងនោះទៅឲ្យកូនក្តីរបស់ លោក/
លោកស្រី ឬ អ្នកដទៃបានឡើយ ។

។

+

ស

៖ តាមរយៈការចូលរ្លួមរបស់កូនក្តីខ្ពួនជាដើមបណ្តឹង
. ការសម្ងាត់របស់តុលាការ និងវិជ្ជាជីវៈ
ទាំងមេធាវីនិងកួនក្តីរបស់ខ្ជួនអាចមានសិទ្ធិសុំពិនិត្យព័ត៍មានសម្ងាត់ ដែលមាន
រដ្ឋប្បវេណី
ប៉ុន្តែមានតែចៅក្រមរបស់អង្គជំនុំជម្រះវិសាមញ្ញប៉ុណ្ណោះ ដែលអាច
នៅក្នងសំណុំរឿង ។
សម្រេចដាក់ព័ត៌មានទាំងនោះសម្រាប់ផ្សព្វផ្សាយជាសាធារណៈ ។ លោក/លោកស្រីជាប់នូវ
កាតព្វកិច្ចមិនឲ្យផ្សព្វផ្សាយព័ត៍មានសម្ងាត់ជាសាធារណៈនេះទេ ។

ឃ.ការដាក់ឯកសារ ៖ ឯកសារផ្លូវការទាំងអស់ត្រូវដាក់តាមរយះមន្ត្រីគ្រប់គ្រងសំណុំរឿង ដួច

មានចែងក្នុងសេចក្តីណែនាំអនុវត្តស្តពីការដាក់ឯកសារ
លោក/លោកស្រីនឹងទទួលបាននូវការជួនដំណឹង
ជាទម្រង់អេឡិចត្រូនិកអំពីឯកសារដែលបានដាក់នៅក្នុងសំណុំរឿង = ហេតុដូច្នេះលោក/
លោកស្រីត្រូវផ្តល់នូវអ៊ីមែល (E-mail) របស់ខ្លួនទៅក្រឡាបញ្ជីអង្គជំនុំជម្រះសាលាដំបូង ។

។

៣

សេចក្តីសម្រេចទទួលស្គាល់មេធាវី គង់ សំអុន/ថ្ងៃទី ១៨ ខែ វិច្ឆិកា ឆ្នាំ ២០១១/សាធារណៈ

វ

£4064

យើងអាចផ្តល់នូវការជួនដំណឹងជាទម្រង់ក្រដាស

ប្រសិនបើអ្នកមិនអាចប្រើប្រាស់អ៊ីមែល

In response to the request by Duch’s lawyers on 23 August 2007, the Co-
Investigating Judges have authorized the latter to consult the case file and obtain a
copy of the Introductory Submission in Khmer and French, with the exception of
the footnotes.

In their observations dated 13 September 2007, Duch’s lawyers claim that the Co-
Prosecutors’ request does not satisfy the level of precision required by the order
of 30 July 2007 and is partly unfounded, as regards the confidentiality of the
judicial investigation due to the fact that the defence already has knowledge of the
witnesses’ names. In consequence, they ask the Co-Investigating Judges to reject
the request and reiterate the right of the defence to obtain an unexpurgated copy
of the case file.

4.

REASONS FOR THE DECISION

The Law

5.

First and foremost, it is important to note that the Co-Prosecutors’ request intends
to accord the benefit of protective measures to persons who have not yet
participated in the proceedings and can be qualified as “potential witnesses”. Yet,
Rule 29 of the Internal Rules aims to guarantee “the protection of Victims who
participate in the proceedings, whether as complainants or Civil Parties, or

Judges are able to take into account any perception of danger or real risk. Until
now, no specific risk has been established for the interviewed persons.

In support of their request, the Co-Prosecutors raise a number of legal arguments
founded on the case law of the ad hoc tribunals and the International Criminal
Court. From this case law, it appears that the practice of the ad hoc tribunals
concerning the protection of victims and witnesses varies widely depending on
the specific provisions of their statute, the local security situation and other
particular factors to each case.

Yet, beyond these apparent differences, there is a strong tendency in the
legislation and international practice that permits the identification of minimum
criteria concerning the
protection of victims and witnesses: before these
international tribunals, except the Special Court for Sierra Leone, the granting of
generalised protective measures is wholly exceptional. Therefore, our order of 30
July 2007 complies with the majority practice of the ad hoc tribunals when
allowing for protective measures in favour of designated persons on the basis of a
specified danger. The request does not reach the required level of specificity since
it simply repeats the demand for general protection of every person mentioned in
the Introductory Submission and in the annexed documents until a full risk
analysis has been conducted, without saying how the absence of protective
measures will place the life or security of each of these persons in grave danger.

It is also important to stress that, since the procedural system applicable before the
ECCC is very different from the system applicable before the international
tribunals, the scope of the case law of the ad hoc tribunals and the International
Criminal Court is necessarily limited.

Accordingly, the analysis by the ICTY Trial Chamber in the Blaskic case (“the
victims and witnesses merit protection, even from the accused, during the
preliminary proceedings and continuing until a reasonable time before the start
of the trial itself; from that time forth, however, the right of the accused to an
equitable trial must take precedence’) cannot be purely and simply transposed to
the ECCC. The concept of the “start of the trial” after which the exercise of
defence rights must be guaranteed, is not identical here, the respect for the rights
of the defence being required from the beginning of the judicial investigation.

2

10.

Equally, a number of the protective measures that have been applied by the
international tribunals, and requested by the Co-Prosecutors, are totally unsuited
to the procedural system applicable before the ECCC. For example, it would be
paradoxical at the very least to order the disclosure of the witnesses’ identity to
the defence “not less than 21 days before” their appearance, because, in this
system, the Co-Investigating Judges only inform the defence of a witness
interview after his or her appearance.

The facts

11.

12.

13,

14.

In support of their request, the Co-Prosecutors develop a number of factual
arguments concerning security risks that justify the granting of protective
measures. They consider that that the Co-Investigating Judges have referred to the
still fragile context of Cambodian society and have made an analogy with the
situation in Sierra Leone.

While it is true that the Co-Investigating Judges reiterated, in their two detention
orders, that the context in Cambodia is still fragile, this was with reference to the
possible release of the charged persons. Since these persons are detained,
however, the need to adopt special protective measures for witnesses is evidently
less compelling.

On the second point, it should be noted that by sole reference to the objective
criteria applied by the United Nations for the establishment of security phases,
Cambodia is currently at level zero while Sierra Leone was at level 3 at the time
when proceedings began in that country. Furthermore, it is important to remember
that the complete surrender of the Khmer Rouge occurred in 1998, years before
the beginning of the ECCC proceedings, and that the crimes within the
jurisdiction of the court with respect to which the witnesses and victims could be
called to testify were committed more than thirty years ago.

The Co-Prosecutors strive to demonstrate the existence of fear and reluctance to
testify before the ECCC (and, more generally, before the Cambodian courts) by
referring to two studies by the Documentation Center of Cambodia (DC-CAM)
and the Centre for Social Development (CSD) respectively. Without questioning
the relevance of these studies, the Co-Investigating Judges note that the examples
quoted do not appear to be decisive because they concern very reduced samples.
Thus, the fact that “12 out of 23 interviewed persons were afraid to appear as a
witness”® cannot suffice to demonstrate the existence of risks for the security of
potential witnesses and victims in general. Similarly, the fact that one of these

15.

10.

It is important to stress that the WESU Unit has indicated that the Co-Prosecutors’
request does not totally reflect its own conclusions, which tend to show that a
certain number of witnesses were perfectly ready to testify publicly and that, for
the majority, the risk level was low. This impression is strongly supported by the
fact that some of the potential witnesses mentioned are already well known to the
public and to the charged persons, through their earlier public declarations in the
media, and that for those who have been interviewed by DC-CAM, their names
have already been made available to the whole world on internet. It can hardly be
asserted that to see these names appear in the case file as potential witnesses
creates a supplementary danger for the persons mentioned.

In consideration of these elements, the Co-Investigating Judges can only state that
the new request by the Co-Prosecutors fails to specify precisely enough the
identity of the persons who should benefit from protective measures. Nor does it
give any evidence of risks that could place the persons mentioned in the case file
in grave danger, as required by our Order of 30 July 2007. This request, which
demands the implementation of protective measures of a general nature for the
benefit of persons who “may” appear before the ECCC, would considerably limit
the exercise of defence rights at this stage of the judicial proceedings. The judicial
investigation is confidential, which is protective but also respectful of the rights of
the defence.

17.

Furthermore, considering the advice given by the Witness/Experts Support
Section, there is no reason to maintain the prohibition of copies of the case file
which had been decided provisionally. Indeed, under Rule 55(6) of the Internal
Rules, the charged person has the right to the necessary time and facilities to
prepare his defence and his lawyers must be able to access the case file and obtain
a copy of it “at all times”.

18.

Accordingly, the Prosecution request is unfounded and the request for a copy of
the case file by Duch’s lawyers is allowed, while still maintaining the terms of our
order of 30 July 2007 concerning the prohibition to disclose any information
obtained through the consultation of the case file, and particularly the names and
any information permitting the identification or localization of the witnesses.

For these reasons

យើង យួ ប៊ុនឡេង និង Marcel Lemonde សហចៅក្រមស៊ើបអង្កេតនៃអង្គជំនុំជម្រះវិសាមញ្ញក្នុងតុលាការកម្ពជា
បានឃើញច្បាប់ស្ដឺពីការបង្កើតអង្គជំនុំជម្រះវិសាមញ្ញ ចុះថ្ងៃទី២៧ ខែតុលា ឆ្នាំ២០០៤
បានឃើញវិធាន ២២ នៃវិធានផ្ទៃក្នុងនៃអង្គជំនុំជម្រះវិសាមញ្ញ

Decision on Protective measures

Ordonnance relative aux mesures de protection

We You Bunleng and Marcel Lemonde Co Investigating Judges of the Extraordinary Chambers in
the Courts of Cambodia

Having seen the Law on the Establishment of the Extraordinary Chambers dated October 27 2004

Having seen Rules 29 and 55 of the Internal Rules of the Extraordinary Chambers

Having seen the judicial investigation opened against KAING GUECK EAV alias DUCH

Having seen the request for protective measures submitted by the Co Prosecutors within the
Introductory Submission dated July 18 2007 paragraphs 119 120 121 and 125

REASONS FOR THE DECISION

The request demands that protective measures be ordered with respect to victims witnesses and third
persons who are referred to
in this Introductory
Submission the accompanying case file and any other material provided therewith
The Co
Prosecutors consider that these measures should include in particular the prohibition of defence
access to the list of potential witnesses
until such time as a comprehensive specific individual
assessment is undertaken

either directly or by necessary implication

The request does not specify the names of the individuals concerned nor how the failure to provide
protective measures would endanger the lives and security of the hundreds of victims witnesses or
third persons mentioned in the Introductory Submission and in its annexes 14000 pages Moreover
it does not explain the reasons why an analysis of the real risks entailed by some of these individuals
was not done during the long preliminary inquiry preceding the opening of the judicial investigation

It is clear that the Co Investigating Judges must take all appropriate measures to ensure the protection
of witnesses and victims However they must not do so indiscriminately
that is to say they cannot
do so without a clear justification and for an unlimited number of individuals They must also
guarantee the rights of the defence which are fundamental rights in a criminal trial and which include

 in particular the right of the lawyers for the Charged Person to have access to the case file and
annexed documents

the request as drafted cannot be accepted However in the current
Given these considerations
absence of any effective measures and during the period needed to consult the units responsible for the
protection of witnesses and victims in order to determine whether it may be necessary to order
protective measures for certain victims and witnesses the delivery of copies of the case file to the
lawyers for the parties must be denied In addition they must be reminded that they are bound by
professional secrecy and must not divulge the contents of any information obtained from consulting the
case file to any person in any manner whatsoever in particular the names and addresses of the
witnesses under penalty of disciplinary and criminal sanctions

FOR THESE REASONS

1

2

3

4

5

6

Partially deny the request for protective measures dated July 18 2007
Allow the lawyers for the Charged Person to consult the entire case file and its annexed
documents for the preparation of his defence
Prohibit provisionally and until completion of a detailed evaluation of the risk incurred by all
individuals mentioned in the Introductory Submission and the annexed documents the delivery
of copies of the case file to the lawyers for the parties
Recall that everyone who has access to the case file is prohibited from disclosing publicly in
any manner whatsoever any information obtained through consultation of the case file
especially the names and relevant information that would allow the identification and location
of the witnesses under penalty of disciplinary or criminal sanctions in accordance with Rules
35 and 38 of the Internal Rules of the Extraordinary Chambers
State that for the purposes of this order the term public means any physical person or legal
entity government organization entity group and media and that it includes in particular the
family friends and relatives of the Charged Person moreover the term
and
annexed documents means any information statements minutes reports photos or other
items submitted whether in paper or electronic format
State that the present order does not preclude any party or person from requesting
in
accordance with Rule 29 of the Internal Rules any protective measures they deem to be
necessary but that in support of such request they must specify

case file

a

the identity of the witnesses victims or third persons who would benefit from these
measures and

b how in the absence of the measures the lives and health of these witnesses victims

their families or their kin would be placed in grave danger

This order was drafted in Khmer and French and subsequently translated into English

Done in Phnom Penh July 30 2007

Nous You Bunleng et Marcel Lemonde co juges d instruction des Chambres extraordinaires

au sein des tribunaux cambodgiens

Vu la Loi sur la création des Chambres extraordinaires en date du 27 octobre 2004

Vu les Règles 29 et 55 du Règlement intérieur des Chambres extraordinaires

Vu l instruction ouverte contre KANG KHEK IEV alias DUCH

Vu la demande de mesures de protection formée par les co procureurs par réquisitoire introductif
paragraphes 1 19 120 121 et 125 en date du 18 uillet 2007

MOTIFS DE LA DECISION

La demande tend à ce que soient ordonnées des mesures de protection à l égard « des victimes des
témoins et des tiers auxquels il est fait référence directement ou indirectement dans le réquisitoire
introductif dans le dossier joint et dans tout autre document produit » Selon les co procureurs ces
mesures devraient notamment comprendre l interdiction pour la défense d accéder à la liste des témoins
potentiels « tant qu une analyse complète des risques encourus n a pas étéfaite »

La demande ne précise ni les noms des personnes concernées ni en quoi l absence de mesures de
protection mettrait en danger grave la vie ou la sécurité des centaines de « victimes témoins ou tiers »
mentionnes dans le réquisitoire introductif et dans ses annexes
Elle n indique pas
davantage ce qui a pu empêcher une analyse des risques réellement encourus par certaines de ces
personnes pendant la longue enquête préliminaire qui a précédé l ouverture de l instruction

14 000 pages

Les co juges d instruction doivent évidemment prendre toutes mesures utiles pour assurer la protection
des témoins ou des victimes Ils ne sauraient toutefois le faire sans discernement c est à dire sans
justification précise et pour un nombre illimité de personnes Ils doivent également garantir les droits de
la défense qui sont des droits fondamentaux dans le procès pénal et qui comprermen^eiSiS^ler le

Au vu de ces éléments il ne peut être fait droit à la demande telle qu elle est formulée En revanche en
l absence de mesures effectives en place et pendant le temps nécessaire à la consultation des unités
chargées de la protection des victimes et témoins sur l éventuelle nécessité de prendre des mesures de
protection pour certains d entre eux il est nécessaire d interdire à titre provisoire la délivrance de
copies du dossier aux avocats des parties et de rappeler à ceux ci qu ils sont liés par le secret
professionnel et qu ils ne peuvent en aucune façon divulguer à qui que ce soit sous peine de sanctions
disciplinaires et pénales toute information obtenue par la consultation du dossier et notamment les
noms et adresses de témoins

Par ces motifs

Rejetons partiellement la demande de mesures de protection en date du 18 juillet 2007
Autorisons les avocats de la personne mise en examen pour la préparation de la défense à
consulter intégralement le dossier et les documents annexes
Interdisons à titre provisoire et jusqu à évaluation détaillée des risques encourus par les
personnes mentionnées au réquisitoire introductif et dans les documents annexes
toute
délivrance de copies du dossier aux avocats des parties
Rappelons qu il est interdit à toute personne ayant accès au dossier de divulguer au public par
quelque moyen que ce soit sous peine de sanctions disciplinaires et pénales conformément aux
règles 35 et 38 du Règlement intérieur des Chambres Extraordinaires toute information obtenue
les informations permettant
par la consultation du dossier et notamment les noms et
l identification ou la localisation des témoins
Disons que dans la présente ordonnance le terme « public » s entend de toutes personnes
et qu il
physiques ou morales gouvernements organisations
comprend en particulier la famille les amis et les relations de la personne mise en examen par
les termes « dossier » et « documents annexes » s entendent de toutes informations
ailleurs
en version papier ou

déclarations
électronique
Disons que la présente ordonnance n empêche aucunement toute partie ou personne de
demander conformément à la règle 29 du Règlement intérieur les mesures de protection qu elle
jugerait nécessaires mais que à l appui de cette demande il devra être précisé

photos ou autres données

procès verbaux rapports

groupes et médias

entités

a

l identité des témoins victimes ou tiers devant bénéficier de ces mesures
en quoi l absence de telles mesures mettrait la vie et la santé de ces témoins victimes de
leurs familles ou proches parents en danger grave

Note des co juges d instruction

Afin de statuer sur la demande de mesures de protection présentée par les co
procureurs conformément à la Règle 29 3 du Règlement intérieur des CETC une
réunion de consultation a été organisée le 26 juillet 2007 avec le Bureau de
l administration

Participaient à cette réunion

le juge d instruction international Marcel Lemonde
la Coordinatrice de l Unité de soutien aux témoins et Experts WESU Wendy
Lobwein

le Chef de la Sécurité Tomas Gerstenecker
l Officier juridique du Directeur Adjoint du Bureau de l administration
Giovanni Bassu
un enquêteur du Bureau de co juges d instruction Steven Heder

Après un exposé des moyens disponibles et des mesures envisageables par le Chef de
la Sécurité et la Coordinatrice de WESU il a été estimé que le bureau des co juges
d instruction pourrait procéder à une révision de la liste des témoins ou témoins
potentiels afin d identifier de possibles sources d information sur la situation de
chacun d eux et en faire part à la Coordinatrice de WESU Cette Unité procéderait
alors à un recensement de divers points de vue afin d obtenir un tableau général du
niveau de risques encourus par les témoins Le Chef de la Sécurité coopérerait avec
WESU en fournissant les rapports de sécurité disponibles

Fait à Phnom Penh le 30 juillet 2007
